cubes.title: Structuring a FastAPI project into modules
rules: Separate concerns into core, models, schemas, api, services, and repositories directories
Use pydantic-settings for configuration in core/config.py
Place route handlers in api/v1/ for versioning
Implement business logic in services/ and data access in repositories/
cubes.rationale: A modular structure improves maintainability, scalability, and collaboration in FastAPI projects.

cubes.title: Three-layer file structure for knowledge base projects
rules: Place all executable code in src/ directory
Store reusable knowledge in knowledge_library/ with subdirectories cubes and anketa
Keep dynamic user-specific data in runtime/ with subdirectories personas, sessions, storage
cubes.rationale: This separation improves modularity, security, and maintainability by keeping code separate from public knowledge and private runtime data.

cubes.title: Project file backup and organization before refactoring
rules: Create a dated backup of the current file using cp file.py file.py.backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)
Keep only the latest working version in the active directory, removing older backups after refactoring and testing
Move old versions to a dedicated archive/ folder using mkdir -p archive && mv file.py.backup-* archive/
Archive by category by creating subdirectories inside archive/ for each file type or module
Use version control (Git) as part of the process
cubes.rationale: To avoid losing work and enable easy rollback during refactoring

cubes.title: Bypass docker-compose KeyError: ContainerConfig by manual container recreation
rules: Forcefully remove the broken container using `docker rm -f <container_name_or_id>`
Identify required settings from docker-compose.yml (image, ports, volumes, env, network, etc.)
Recreate the container with `docker run` using the identified settings, explicitly passing all options
cubes.rationale: When docker-compose fails with KeyError: 'ContainerConfig', the container's metadata is corrupted or incompatible. Manually recreating the container with docker run bypasses the corrupted metadata and restores functionality.

cubes.title: Reflex Cache Architecture
rules: Session-level cache for Discovery: cache a discovery for the entire user session once loaded.
Dialogue-level cache for Search Results: cache search results (brief info) within the current dialogue; reuse without re-executing search.
Lazy loading of Full Cubes: return only brief info initially; fetch full cube only when AI decides to apply it.
Prohibition of reloading already-loaded rules: once cached, a rule cannot be reloaded to avoid duplicate processing.
cubes.rationale: The reflex cache architecture is an automatic, rule-driven caching strategy triggered by keywords to minimize token usage and optimize response speed. It operates as a layered caching system (session-level for discoveries, dialogue-level for search results, lazy loading for full cubes) and prohibits reloading cached rules, thereby reducing latency and cost.

cubes.title: Implementing Rate Limiting in FastAPI using slowapi
rules: Install slowapi: pip install slowapi
Create Limiter instance with key_func=get_remote_address
Add limiter to app.state
Add exception handler for RateLimitExceeded
Use @limiter.limit decorator on endpoints
cubes.rationale: Rate limiting protects FastAPI applications from abuse, ensures fair usage, and prevents resource exhaustion.
